author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
Against the Title of the Epistle of Manichæus.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Against the Title of the Epistle of Manichæus.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Let us see then what Manichæus teaches me; and particularly let us examine that treatise which he calls the Fundamental Epistle, in which almost all that you believe is contained.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For in that unhappy time when we read it we were in your opinion enlightened.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: The epistle begins thus:—"Manichæus, an apostle of Jesus Christ, by the providence of God the Father.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: These are wholesome words from the perennial and living fountain."

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Now, if you please, patiently give heed to my inquiry.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: I do not believe Manichæus to be an apostle of Christ.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Do not, I beg of you, be enraged and begin to curse.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For you know that it is my rule to believe none of your statements without consideration.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Therefore I ask, who is this Manichæus?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: You will reply, An apostle of Christ.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: I do not believe it.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Now you are at a loss what to say or do; for you promised to give knowledge of the truth, and here you are forcing me to believe what I have no knowledge of.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Perhaps you will read the gospel to me, and will attempt to find there a testimony to Manichæus.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But should you meet with a person not yet believing the gospel, how would you reply to him were he to say, I do not believe?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For my part, I should not believe the gospel except as moved by the authority of the Catholic Church.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: So when those on whose authority I have consented to believe in the gospel tell me not to believe in Manichæus, how can I but consent?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Take your choice.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: If you say, Believe the Catholics: their advice to me is to put no faith in you; so that, believing them, I am precluded from believing you;—If you say, Do not believe the Catholics: you cannot fairly use the gospel in bringing me to faith in Manichæus; for it was at the command of the Catholics that I believed the gospel;—Again, if you say, You were right in believing the Catholics when they praised the gospel, but wrong in believing their vituperation of Manichæus: do you think me such a fool as to believe or not to believe as you like or dislike, without any reason?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: It is therefore fairer and safer by far for me, having in one instance put faith in the Catholics, not to go over to you, till, instead of bidding me believe, you make me understand something in the clearest and most open manner.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: To convince me, then, you must put aside the gospel.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: If you keep to the gospel, I will keep to those who commanded me to believe the gospel; and, in obedience to them, I will not believe you at all.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But if haply you should succeed in finding in the gospel an incontrovertible testimony to the apostleship of Manichæus, you will weaken my regard for the authority of the Catholics who bid me not to believe you; and the effect of that will be, that I shall no longer be able to believe the gospel either, for it was through the Catholics that I got my faith in it; and so, whatever you bring from the gospel will no longer have any weight with me.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Wherefore, if no clear proof of the apostleship of Manichæus is found in the gospel, I will believe the Catholics rather than you.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But if you read thence some passage clearly in favor of Manichæus, I will believe neither them nor you: not them, for they lied to me about you; nor you, for you quote to me that Scripture which I had believed on the authority of those liars.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But far be it that I should not believe the gospel; for believing it, I find no way of believing you too.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For the names of the apostles, as there recorded, do not include the name of Manichæus.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And who the successor of Christ’s betrayer was we read in the Acts of the Apostles; which book I must needs believe if I believe the gospel, since both writings alike Catholic authority commends to me.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: The same book contains the well-known narrative of the calling and apostleship of Paul.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Read me now, if you can, in the gospel where Manichæus is called an apostle, or in any other book in which I have professed to believe.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Will you read the passage where the Lord promised the Holy Spirit as a Paraclete, to the apostles?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Concerning which passage, behold how many and how great are the things that restrain and deter me from believing in Manichæus.
